title: ConversationTurn
description: One turn in a multi-turn conversation. Structural superset of `ConversationMessage` (RFC 0002 §G — append-only, idempotent on `messageId`, replay-deterministic). Adds `role`, optional `agent` reference, and `turnIndex` discriminators. Folds through the existing `applyMessage` reducer unchanged. See RFC 0005 §C.

messageId.description: Dedup key. Producers MUST construct a deterministic id so re-folding is idempotent. Recommended encoding: `${conversationId}:${turnIndex}:${role}` (e.g., `run-abc:n1:0:7:agent`).

from.description: Sender identity. Either an agent ID (RFC 0002 §A1) or the literal `'user'` for human-originated turns.

to.description: Optional addressee. Absent = broadcast to the conversation's group (or the run's default participants).

groupId.description: Optional shared-agent group ID. When set, the turn is scoped to the `shared:<groupId>` agent context per RFC 0002 §A8.

content.description: Opaque turn content. Protocol does not standardize the shape; hosts use Zod schemas at the application layer for structured turns. Pass-through for any JSON value.

ts.description: Turn timestamp (ms epoch). Caller's clock; replay carries through unchanged for determinism.

role.description: Discriminator distinguishing human-originated turns (`'user'`), AI-agent-originated turns (`'agent'`), and host-emitted procedural turns (`'system'` — e.g., `'conversation timed out'`).

agent.description: Optional agent reference (RFC 0002 §A1 `AgentRef`) for `role: 'agent'` turns. The same shape RFC 0002 reserves on `RunSnapshot.agent` and `node.started.payload.agent` — propagated through inheritance.

agentSharing.description: Per RFC 0002 §A8 — `shared:<groupId>` requires a `groupId` field on the message.

memoryRef.description: Opaque memory reference per RFC 0002 §A + RFC 0004 §A. Resolves to `MemoryEntry[]` via host `MemoryAdapter` for cross-run conversation continuity.

turnIndex.description: 0-based index within the conversation. `conversation.opened.initialTurn` carries `turnIndex: 0`; subsequent `conversation.exchanged` turns increment monotonically; `conversation.closed.finalTurn` carries the highest index.
